commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: A vow is a request for a benefit from God with a promise to give something in return. Hence, when you have obtained what you sought, it would be an ungrateful act to delay what you have promised. .

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Delay, beyond the time appointed. (Menochius)
If no time was specified, the vow must be fulfilled without any unnecessary procrastination. See Numbers xxx. 2. (Haydock)
Vows induce an obligation which before did not exist. (Worthington)
